title: Mixed Mode Test
description: All question types in one quiz

questions.question: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.explanation: Canberra has been the capital of Australia since 1913.

questions.question: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.
questions.explanation: This is a common myth. The Great Wall is too narrow to be seen from space without aid.

questions.question: Which of these animals can fly?
questions.explanation: Bats are the only mammals capable of true flight. One answer was fake!

questions.question: When was the Berlin Wall torn down?
questions.explanation: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989.

questions.question: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level.
questions.explanation: Water boils at exactly 100°C (212°F) at standard atmospheric pressure at sea level.

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
questions.explanation: Au comes from the Latin word 'aurum'.

questions.question: Which planet has the most moons?
questions.explanation: Saturn has over 140 confirmed moons, surpassing Jupiter.

questions.question: When was the first manned Moon landing?
questions.explanation: Apollo 11 landed on the Moon on July 20, 1969.

questions.question: Honey never spoils.
questions.explanation: Archaeologists have found 3000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still edible!

questions.question: Name the largest ocean on Earth.
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean covers about 165.25 million km².

questions.question: Which country invented pizza?
questions.explanation: Modern pizza originated in Naples, Italy in the 18th century.

questions.question: Bananas are berries, but strawberries are not.
questions.explanation: Botanically, bananas qualify as berries while strawberries are 'accessory fruits'.

questions.question: When did World War II end?
questions.explanation: WWII ended on September 2, 1945 with Japan's formal surrender.

questions.question: What is the tallest mountain in the world?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest stands at 8,849 meters above sea level.

questions.question: What is the fastest land animal?
questions.explanation: Cheetahs can reach speeds of up to 120 km/h.
